stories.title: UK Navy Says Ship in Gulf of Oman Taken, Heading to Iran
stories.summary: A UK naval group reported a commercial vessel was taken by unauthorized personnel at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz and is now bound for Iranian waters. The ship was taken 38 nautical miles off the UAE in the Gulf of Oman where it was at anchor, the UK Maritime Trade Operations said in a statement. Bloomberg’s Anthony di Paola has more.

stories.title: US Says China Wants More Of Its Oil to Cut Middle East Reliance
stories.summary: A White House official said that Chinese President Xi Jinping opposes efforts to charge a toll to navigate the Strait of Hormuz and is interested in purchasing more US oil to reduce its dependence on the waterway in the future.

stories.title: US Efforts to End Iran War Stumble as Ship Seized Near UAE
stories.summary: US efforts to end the war with Iran were dealt a setback after a commercial vessel was apparently seized by unauthorized personnel near the United Arab Emirates, increasing uncertainty over control of the critical Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump-Xi Summit: Lavish Welcome, Taiwan in Focus
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned US President Donald Trump of a potential conflict if the Taiwan issue is mismanaged, in blunt remarks that punctured an otherwise cordial start to the first trip to China by a sitting US president in nearly a decade. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump-Xi Summit: Xi Warns Trump Of ‘Clashes’ If Taiwan Issue Mishandled | Daybreak Europe 05/14/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins. Chinese President Xi Jinping pressed US President Donald Trump on the issue of Taiwan and warned of a potentia

stories.title: UK navy says vessel seized off UAE coast and heading for Iran
stories.summary: Ship anchored north-east of Fujairah outside Strait of Hormuz taken by ‘unauthorised personnel’

stories.title: Trade wars to extended truce: Analysts expect ‘stabilization’ in U.S.-China ties as Trump-Xi meet
stories.summary: The world's two largest economies sought to stabilize fractured ties at their first high-level meeting in nearly a decade.

stories.title: Geely, Xiaomi lead the pack: battery EVs top China sales on rising oil costs
stories.summary: Surging oil prices are prompting Chinese buyers to shift to battery electric vehicles (BEVs), which dominated April sales rankings by taking nine out of the top 10 spots – a new high in China’s electrification drive. Only one petrol car model was among the bestsellers across all power trains, with Geely’s Binyue in eighth place on sales of 14,923 units, according to data from automotive platform D

stories.title: UN Climate Chief Lavishes Praise on China as Trump Meets Xi
stories.summary: China is reaping benefits as a global leader on the green transition and other nations should follow as the Iran war shows the vulnerability of a fossil fuel-based economy, according to the top United Nations climate official.

stories.title: Cuba says oil and diesel supplies have run dry under U.S. sanctions
stories.summary: A Cuban official said the situation is “extremely tense” as blackouts sparked protests in Havana on Wednesday.

stories.title: Ship Taken in Gulf of Oman and Heading to Iran, UK Navy Says
stories.summary: A UK naval group reported a commercial vessel was taken by unauthorized personnel at the entrance to the Strait of Hormuz and is now bound for Iranian waters, the latest indication of Tehran exerting control on shipping in the region.

stories.title: Xi Tells US CEOs Accompanying Trump That China Will Open Up More
stories.summary: Xi Jinping signaled China is moving toward greater openness, striking an upbeat note during his meeting with US business leaders accompanying Donald Trump as the two presidents wrapped up morning talks that featured more divisive issues like trade and Taiwan.

stories.title: India Faces Challenge of Bridging BRICS Rift Over Iran War
stories.summary: India faces the challenge of bridging deep divisions within the BRICS bloc gathering in New Delhi this week, as members remain split over the Middle East conflict and China’s focus is diverted by President Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing.

stories.title: Trump-Xi Meeting Focuses on Taiwan Issue
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping pressed US President Donald Trump on the issue of Taiwan and warned of a potential conflict if mismanaged. The two leaders met Thursday morning at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing following an elaborate welcome ceremony during the first visit to China by a sitting US president in nearly a decade. Bloomberg's Minmin Low reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Adtek Is Said to Explore Hong Kong IPO at $4 Billion Valuation
stories.summary: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a wave of Chinese listings by businesses related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai Profit Jumps With AI Sales Boost
stories.summary: Nvidia Corp.’s major server assembly partner Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a stronger-than-expected increase in quarterly profit, highlighting sustained spending on hardware essential for AI.

stories.title: Cerebras Raises $5.55 Billion in Year’s Biggest IPO
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence chipmaker Cerebras Systems Inc. raised $5.55 billion in its US initial public offering. The company priced its shares at $185 each, giving Cerebras a market value of about $40 billion, based on the shares offered and outstanding in its filings. Bloomberg’s Neil Campling reports.

stories.title: US-China relations depend on Taiwan, Xi warns Trump
stories.summary: Chinese president tells US counterpart there are ‘no winners’ in trade war

stories.title: What Xi and Trump want from their Beijing summit
stories.summary: Chinese and US leaders to discuss trade, technology, Iran and Taiwan

stories.title: ECB to Hike If Oil Deanchors CPI Expectations, Kazaks Says
stories.summary: The European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs if an increase in crude prices feeds through to inflation expectations, according to Governing Council member Martins Kazaks.

stories.title: Americans still feel bad about the economy. When will it get better?
stories.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment has been on a downward trend since the Covid-19 pandemic. Inflation, wars and President Trump's tariffs are to blame, economists say.

stories.title: Xi, Trump Project Pragmatism as Tension Simmers
stories.summary: As the leaders work to preserve fragile truces in Beijing, issues such as Taiwan and trade lurk.

stories.title: UK economy sees surprise growth in March despite Iran war
stories.summary: The economy grew by 0.3% in the month, official figures show, confounding analysts' forecasts of a small contraction.

stories.title: Singapore Airlines Net Profit Dragged Down by Air India Woes
stories.summary: Singapore Airlines Ltd. posted a drop in annual net profit as widening losses at Air India weighed on results amid the broader impact of the Iran war on the aviation industry.

stories.title: Franklin CEO Says Markets Are Underestimating Inflation
stories.summary: Franklin Templeton Chief Executive Officer Jenny Johnson says that the US economy is "still very healthy" and there are reasons for the "euphoria" around markets. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Johnson also warns of inflation risks, saying that "the reality of inflation is it's sticky" and markets are underestimating the reality of it. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Iran war fallout forces Singapore Airlines-backed Air India to cancel 27% of international flights
stories.summary: Air India, a carrier co-owned by Tata Group and Singapore Airlines, plans to scale back over a quarter of its international flights from June as rising costs bite.

stories.title: Sapporo Halts Exports of Pokka Brand to Mideast Amid War Snags
stories.summary: Sapporo Holdings Ltd. suspended exports of its Pokka brand of beverages to the Middle East as escalating tensions involving Iran heighten concerns about demand.

stories.title: Oil rises as IEA flags greater volatility ahead, OPEC cuts demand forecast
stories.summary: OPEC cut its demand growth estimates for 2026 to about 1.2 million barrels per day.

stories.title: UK grows 0.6% in the first quarter — before the Iran war really started to hit global economy
stories.summary: The data will provide a ray of comfort for the Labour government, which is under pressure.

stories.title: Turkey Scraps Inflation Target in Face of Energy Price Shock
stories.summary: Turkey’s central bank presented a bigger than expected revision to its year-end inflation target and suspended the use of a forecast range, citing major uncertainty precipitated by the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: UK Posts Strongest Growth in a Year Even as Headwinds Build
stories.summary: Panmure Liberum economist Simon French discusses the UK economy and the political crisis surrounding Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Gross domestic product grew 0.6% in the first quarter, a jump from the 0.2% in the previous three months and the fastest expansion for a year. French speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump-Xi summit revives China tech rally hopes as U.S. reportedly clears Nvidia H200 sales
stories.summary: Market watchers are betting that the Trump-Xi summit could extend trade truce and lift Chinese equities.

stories.title: Goldman Now Sees Two 2026 South Africa Rate Hikes Because of War
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. expects South Africa to raise interest rates twice this year after previously seeing a series of cuts, as the Iran war fans inflation risks around the world.

stories.title: Chinese chip ETFs pause trading as they warn of soaring premium risks
stories.summary: Two China-domiciled exchange-traded funds (ETFs) targeting overseas chipmakers warned of investment risks arising from excessive premiums to net asset values and imposed a temporary trading halt, as the record-setting run in tech stocks has spurred Chinese investors to chase the global rally. Hutai-PineBridge CSI KRX China-Korea Semiconductor ETF, which invests in Chinese and South Korean chipmake

stories.title: Burberry’s Cautious Outlook Disappoints After Sales Boost
stories.summary: Burberry Group Plc disappointed investors after the British luxury brand issued a cautious outlook for this fiscal year and warned geopolitical and economic uncertainty may hurt shopper confidence.

stories.title: 'Better Than Ever': Trump, Xi Hail Ties at Beijing Summit | The China Show 5/14/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Seeks $2 Billion Loan for Malaysia Growth
stories.summary: Blackstone Inc.-owned data center firm AirTrunk is marketing a $2.3 billion loan to fund a Malaysia project, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a slew of tech-linked financings in Southeast Asia spurred by the artificial intelligence boom.

stories.title: Cerebras prices IPO above expected range, as Wall Street braces for AI tsunami
stories.summary: Cerebras raised $5.55 billion in its IPO, and with the chipmaker's offering, investors are gearing up for some even bigger AI deals later this year.

stories.title: Blackstone REIT Raises $1.75 Billion in IPO to Buy Data Centers
stories.summary: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. raised $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, as investors’ appetite for artificial intelligence infrastructure shows no sign of easing.

stories.title: Gold Holds Steady as Market Weighs Federal Reserve Rate Path
stories.summary: Gold was steady as investors weighed a resurgence in US inflation that reinforced bets the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer.

stories.title: Tech Rally Rolls On as Cisco Highlights AI Fervor: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: The rally in technology stocks is showing little sign of cooling as Cisco Systems Inc.’s earnings and a hot streak in Nvidia Corp. highlighted the momentum around artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Bobi Wine: New Ugandan Law Silences Political Rivals
stories.summary: Ugandan lawmakers have passed a contentious new law that imposes steep fines and jail terms for those receiving foreign funding without state approval. Opposition leader Bobi Wine, who fled the country earlier this year, told Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja the measures are targeting political rivals and cutting off funding, in what he describes as an effort to silence political rivals. Original fi

stories.title: India’s Factory-Gate Inflation Jumps on Higher Energy Prices
stories.summary: India’s producer prices surged to just over a three-and-a-half-year high in April as elevated energy prices pushed up manufacturers’ input costs.

stories.title: China’s Credit Growth Misses Expectations as New Loans Contract
stories.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed far more than expected from a year earlier in April while new loans extended by the country’s banks shrank, hurt by a seasonal slowdown in lending.

stories.title: Fertilizer Firm Eyes More Than $2.5 Billion Value in Oman IPO
stories.summary: A fertilizer producer in Oman is considering plans to raise several hundred million dollars in one of the biggest initial public offerings in the Middle East since the regional war began.

stories.title: Cisco's stock pops 17% on surging AI orders, as company says it's cutting almost 4,000 jobs
stories.summary: Cisco's AI story has finally started resonating with Wall Street, with the stock hitting a record late last year and continuing to rally in 2026.

stories.title: Cuba has run out of diesel and oil, energy minister says
stories.summary: The minister says the situation in Cuba is "extremely tense", as a US-led blockade of oil to the country causes widespread power cuts.

stories.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang on China trip: ‘President Trump asked me to come’
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday landed in Beijing for the high-stakes summit with China.

stories.title: Poland’s Economic Growth Miss Set to Temper Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary: Poland’s economic growth slowed more than anticipated in the first quarter, with the fading momentum set to moderate expectations regarding interest-rate increases amid a flare-up in price pressures.

stories.title: Brookfield Nears $935 Million Loan for World Freight Purchase
stories.summary: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. is close to securing a $935 million loan to back its purchase of air cargo specialist World Freight Co., according to people familiar with the matter, the latest in a recent spate of acquisition financings in Asia.

stories.title: Burberry says turnaround on track as Americas and China fuel strong growth
stories.summary: Full-year revenue came in largely in line with expectations at £2.4 billion.

stories.title: Appian Buys Namibia Copper Project Eyeing $400 Million Mine
stories.summary: Appian Capital Advisory, a leading mining-focused private equity firm, has acquired a copper project in Namibia as it looks to boost output of a metal that’s expected to experience rapid demand growth.

stories.title: Revolut Gets UK Approval to Expand Investments for Customers
stories.summary: Revolut Ltd. has received approval from UK regulators to expand its investment offerings in the country, as the fintech firm seeks to win more wealthy clients.

stories.title: Myanmar Junta’s Forced Remittance Rules Pull in $5.6 Billion
stories.summary: Myanmar has turned migrant remittances into its biggest source of foreign inflows, finance ministry data seen by Bloomberg News show, after the military imposed rules that pressure citizens abroad to send money home.

stories.title: Poll Shows UK Public Divided Over When Starmer Should Resign
stories.summary: New polling by Ipsos shows that two in three Britons believe UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer should not lead the Labour Party into the next general election. But they are divided on whether he should resign immediately or sometime between now and the next election. Keiran Pedley, Director of UK Politics at Ipsos joined Lizzy Burden on Bloomberg Daybreak Europe. He said the public are the most pessi

stories.title: Xi tells Nvidia, Tesla and Apple CEOs that China will ‘open wider’
stories.summary: US business leaders praise ‘great importance’ of Chinese market on visit to Beijing alongside Donald Trump

stories.title: London Office Leasing Rebounds on Demand Fueled by Finance Firms
stories.summary: London office leasing bounced back from Covid-era lows, with tenant floor-space expansions reaching a six-year high on rising demand from finance and professional services firms, according to broker Cushman & Wakefield.

stories.title: Telefónica Shares Jump After Brazil, Spain Drive Profit Growth
stories.summary: Telefónica SA shares jumped the most in almost three months as the Spanish carrier reported higher first-quarter earnings, boosted by growth in Brazil and easing competition in Spain.

stories.title: HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors
stories.summary: Quantexa, a financial data platform, won the £175m contract to spot fraud and tax return errors.

stories.title: India’s Top Banks Failing to Tackle Climate Risk as Threats Grow
stories.summary: India’s largest banks need to do more to incorporate climate risks into lending decisions as the country suffers escalating impacts from the effects of global warming, according to a new analysis.

stories.title: Alibaba shares surge 7% in Hong Kong as firm accelerates pivot to AI
stories.summary: Shares of Alibaba Group Holding opened up 7.8 per cent in Hong Kong, after the firm reported strong growth from its artificial intelligence products and ramped up its commitment to the technology. The Chinese tech giant also saw its New York-listed shares surge 8.2 per cent on Wednesday, closing at US$145.81, after it confirmed that revenues from AI-related products had hit 8.97 billion yuan (US$1

stories.title: Google races to put Gemini at the center of Android before Apple’s AI reboot
stories.summary: Google is using its latest Android rollout to position Gemini as the AI layer across phones, Chrome, laptops and cars.

stories.title: Australian giant Coles misled shoppers with fake discounts, court rules
stories.summary: The decision, which comes amid similar case against Woolworths, could mean significant penalties.

stories.title: Analysis: Trump finally gets his man at the Fed. Will Kevin Warsh disappoint him?
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh received the fewest votes of any Fed chair in history. Will it cripple him?

stories.title: Kevin Warsh wins Senate confirmation as the next Federal Reserve chair
stories.summary: In the most divisive vote ever for a Fed chair, Warsh, 56, won confirmation to take over for Jerome Powell.

stories.title: Hong Kong’s Central office rents set to reverse multi-year slump with gains in second half
stories.summary: Office rents in Hong Kong’s Central district are expected to rise faster in the second quarter of the year, according to analysts, a reversal of a multi-year slump that began in the second half of 2019. The latest forecast was made by US bank Citi, which said premium offices in the city’s main business zone had seen rents rise 1.7 per cent from a trough in October, with room for further increments

stories.title: Microsoft feared being too dependent on OpenAI, Musk-Altman trial testimony reveals
stories.summary: Top Microsoft executives testified in Musk v. Altman this week, spelling out concerns they had in the early days of the partnership with OpenAI.

stories.title: Jensen Huang joins Trump's China trip after the U.S. president called the Nvidia CEO
stories.summary: The U.S. chipmaker executive was not included in earlier lists of business leaders participating in President Donald Trump's trip to China this week.

stories.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang bets on this British startup to build 'next frontier' of AI
stories.summary: Months-old Ineffable Intelligence announced a record $1.1 billion seed round in April.

stories.title: AI-driven cyberattacks will start to be the 'new norm' in months, Palo Alto warns
stories.summary: The rise of increasingly sophisticated AI models is putting pressure on cybersecurity teams to step up their defenses for newer and faster cyberattacks.

stories.title: Trump's Fed chair pick Kevin Warsh confirmed by US Senate
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the narrowest margin since the role required a Senate confirmation vote.

stories.title: Mortgage rates jump to highest level since March on hotter inflation reports
stories.summary: Mortgage rates surged to the highest level since March in reaction to two hotter-than-expected inflation reports.

stories.title: Trump is taking more than a dozen U.S. executives to China. Jensen Huang isn't one of them
stories.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang isn't going on Trump’s China trip, though more than a dozen other U.S. executives are.

stories.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

stories.title: 'Bond markets on edge' as King Charles sets out fragile UK government's agenda
stories.summary: U.K. gilts and bonds faced heavy selling pressure in response to the latest 'Starmer drama', as the prime minister faced down rivals ahead of the King's Speech.

stories.title: Tesla lures budget-conscious Chinese buyers with cheap loans ahead of state visit by Trump
stories.summary: Tesla China has launched a new financing scheme to attract more budget-conscious buyers after its sales in the world’s largest electric vehicle (EV) market fell behind its domestic rivals. The US carmaker said on Wednesday that the down payment for a Shanghai-made Model 3 vehicle – priced at 235,500 yuan (US$34,672) – would be slashed to 55,900 yuan from 79,900 yuan if the buyer chose a five-year

stories.summary: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

stories.summary: Henderson Land Development secured the first biodiversity loan in Hong Kong, receiving around HK$100 million (US$12.8 million) from HSBC and Hang Seng Bank for green initiatives at Central Yards, the company’s flagship mixed-use development on the New Central Harbourfront. stories.summary: London’s Alphonso mango supply is down this year due to fewer imports and higher prices for shoppers.

stories.title: How Australia’s mining giants are helping China to globalise the yuan
stories.summary: Australia’s mining giants are aiding China’s push to internationalise its currency and reduce the dominance of the US dollar, as they gradually shift towards using the yuan for financing and settlements, analysts said.
